reasoning: Tajima Beef (reg #2) and Kobe Beef (reg #3) both appear at the top with official MAFF GI data, accurate descriptions, and GI registration details. Response addresses the Tajima-Kobe relationship correctly. Minor gap: 195 items returned with no keyword filter, so non-beef items fill the remaining payload, diluting focus.
missing_data: No where-to-eat context or farm/production area map. No specific Tajima region (Asago/Yabu) spot links. Visitor information for Kobe Beef restaurants absent.
improvement_hint: Add a keyword or category pre-filter so the top results stay on-topic when the query names a specific GI product; return Tajima/Kobe items first via relevance sort.

reasoning: Arabic language requested and delivered; Tajima Beef and Kobe Beef appear first with correct official MAFF GI descriptions in Arabic at high confidence. Same structural issue as L1-01: 195 records returned with non-beef items. Translation quality looks fluent and accurate.
missing_data: Same gaps as L1-01: no restaurant/visit guidance, no production-area maps for Arabic-speaking travellers.
improvement_hint: Add relevance-ranked filtering so the first page shows only items matching the query topic; improves both English and non-English queries.

reasoning: Query explicitly asks about Minami-Yamashiro village tea fields (not Uji, not Kyoto city landmarks). get_japan_heritage returns Nijo Castle, Toji, Nanzen-ji etc. — all Kyoto city monuments, none relevant to the village or tea cultivation. The tool note even warns about the scope mismatch. Constraint 'just the village, not Uji' entirely ignored.
missing_data: Any data on Minami-Yamashiro village (南山城村) tea fields, their landscape, access, or Japan Heritage or GI designation for local tea.
improvement_hint: Route municipality-scoped queries to get_spots or search_area with municipality='南山城村'; get_japan_heritage with prefecture only is too coarse for village-level constraints.

reasoning: Yoshida Fire Festival found and correctly identified as 重要無形民俗文化財 in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi. Spanish translation present. However the item has date=null, no practical timing/location detail. national_heritage fills with unrelated UNESCO items (Akita, Miyagi, etc.) cluttering the response. Visitor-actionable info thin.
missing_data: Specific date (late Aug 26-27), venue (Kitaguchi Hongu Fuji Sengen Shrine), what the ritual involves, how to get there from the Mt Fuji area.
improvement_hint: Populate date fields for known-annual festivals; add venue coordinates and event description_es from the same translation pass already run on other fields.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about 中芸地域 (Chugei area) yuzu. Response returns 物部ゆず (Monobe, Kami City) as the first GI item — that's Kochi but different sub-region. Subsequent scraped items are irrelevant sightseeing pages (temples, aquariums, arcades) with no yuzu content. 中芸ゆず GI product not found. Count=500 with 651 total, heavily truncated.
missing_data: 中芸ゆず itself (Nahari/Yasuda/Kitagawa area yuzu); processing methods, yuzusco condiments, farm visit info specific to Chugei.
improvement_hint: Add keyword param to get_local_food so 'ゆず 中芸' narrows the 651-item pool; scraped_local_food items should pass a food-relevance filter before inclusion.

reasoning: Query in Chinese asks about Hirosaki Park cherry blossoms. Response returns 弘前城 (must_see, sakura_meisho_100) and 弘前公園 (must_see, sakura_meisho_100, garden/park) with coordinates and phone numbers. Relevant and accurate. Minor: no cherry blossom season dates, no Chinese description of the cherry blossom experience.
missing_data: Bloom season (late Apr–early May), number of trees (~2,600), weeping cherries, crowd advice, shuttle info in Chinese.
improvement_hint: Enrich sakura_meisho_100 spots with seasonal metadata (bloom window, peak week) so seasonal queries can get date-specific answers.

reasoning: Korean query about Izumo Taisha answered with correct primary match (Q696362, Shimane, free admission, National Treasure). Honden sub-structure also present with correct heritage designations. IzumoTaisha-mae station included with useful access note. Minor noise: Chiba branch shrine (出雲大社上総分院) appears but is clearly labeled as Chiba.
missing_data: No Korean-language description of the shrine's significance (en'musubi marriage deity, October gathering of gods). Hours show 06:00-19:00 but seasonal variation not noted.
improvement_hint: Add ko-language description to the primary Izumo Taisha Wikidata entity via the translation pipeline; currently description only in English.

reasoning: French query about Itsukushima Shrine; tool called with lang=en instead of lang=fr — so all descriptions are English, not French. Despite that, correct data: Itsukushima island, shrine (UNESCO WHS, National Treasure), and Otorii gate all present. Factually sound. Constraint failure: language mismatch between query and response.
missing_data: French-language descriptions; tidal gate viewing times; ferry info from Miyajima-guchi.
improvement_hint: Pass lang=fr when the user queries in French; the tool supports multilingual output. Consider language-detection in the routing layer.

reasoning: German query about Kumano Kodo uses get_japan_heritage which is the wrong tool (scope note says to use search_area for pilgrimage routes). Kumano Kodo does appear in top_canonical_heritage (Q1148078) with correct UNESCO designation, which partially saves it. But the main items section fills with Japan Heritage stories unrelated to Kumano Kodo. Limited practical pilgrimage route info.
missing_data: Route segments (Nakahechi, Kohechi, Ohechi), trailhead access, pilgrim stamps, guesthouses along the route, required fitness level.
improvement_hint: When intent classifier detects pilgrimage-route query, route to search_area or get_spots with pilgrimage_route kind tag rather than get_japan_heritage.

reasoning: Russian query about Himeji Castle; response leads with correct primary entry (Q188754): UNESCO WHS, National Treasure, Special Historic Site, accurate coords, opening hours, phone, website, fee, wheelchair=no. Sub-structures (Ha Watariyagura, Northwest Small Keep, etc.) also included with heritage designations. Comprehensive and actionable.
missing_data: No Russian-language description (only English). Cherry blossom season context missing. Audio guide / ticket price details absent.
improvement_hint: Add Russian (ru) to the translation pipeline for top-tier attractions; currently descriptions are English-only for this response despite ru being a supported lang code.

reasoning: Thai query asks specifically about Bunraku in Osaka. Bunraku appears first (UNESCO ICH, 2003) with correct Thai name and description. However the query specifies Osaka as venue — the response has no location data linking Bunraku to the National Bunraku Theatre (国立文楽劇場) in Nipponbashi. All 183 items returned without Osaka filter, flooding with unrelated arts.
missing_data: 国立文楽劇場 address, performance schedule, ticket booking link, how to reach Nipponbashi from Namba/Shinsaibashi.
improvement_hint: After identifying the art form, cross-reference with get_spots or search_area to surface the specific venue (Q-entity for 国立文楽劇場) and link it to the arts record.

reasoning: Vietnamese query asks specifically about Yumihama Kasuri weaving (弓浜絣). The response returns only 7 MAFF GI food items (rakkyo, broccoli, persimmon, watermelon, etc.) for Tottori — no craft or textile items. Yumihama Kasuri is a METI traditional craft, not MAFF GI food, so get_local_specialty with category filter needed. Complete retrieval failure for the intended topic.
missing_data: 弓浜絣 METI craft record, production area (Sakai-minato/Yonago area), workshop visit info, weaving technique description in Vietnamese.
improvement_hint: get_local_specialty should be called without category='food' filter or with category='craft' to surface Yumihama Kasuri; ideally query routing detects textile/craft intent.

reasoning: Filipino query about Naoshima Art Island. Benesse Art Site Naoshima (must_see) appears first. New Museum, municipality entry, and scrape items also included. Solid coverage of art island identity. Weakness: 直島 New Museum coordinates present but Chichu Art Museum (地中美術館) — the most famous spot — not clearly surfaced in the truncated 4000-char window. No ferry info.
missing_data: 地中美術館, 李禹煥美術館 specific entries; ferry timetables from Uno/Takamatsu; art house project (家プロジェクト) overview.
improvement_hint: Ensure Chichu Art Museum and Lee Ufan Museum are indexed as distinct high-prominence Wikidata entities so they rank at the top for Naoshima queries.

reasoning: English query about Kakunodate samurai district answered excellently: primary spot (Q11428562) has rich English description mentioning weeping cherries, correct heritage designation (IPD Traditional Buildings), coordinates, practical metadata (120 min, low price, high crowd). Weeping cherry trees listed separately with Natural Monument. Cherry Bark Work Museum also present. Highly actionable.
missing_data: Seasonal dates for cherry blossom (late Apr), specific individual residences open to public (Aoyagi-ke, Ishiguro-ke), access from Shinkansen stop.
improvement_hint: Minor: add sakura bloom month to sakura_meisho_100 kind defaults so agents can answer seasonal queries without a separate call.

reasoning: Indonesian query about Onomichi, Hiroshima. Shimanami Kaido (cycling route, must_see) leads appropriately; Onomichi Museum of Art with OSM-verified hours included. Onomichi city entity with walking_road_500 and Japan Heritage tags present. However Onomichi Castle is tagged as 'museum' (kind), which is questionable since it's a replica. No cat temple path, no temple walk info.
missing_data: 千光寺 (Senkoji) temple, cat alley (猫の細道), temple walk (坂道), ropeways, and the characteristic hillside lane atmosphere that defines Onomichi tourism.
wrong_data: Onomichi Castle categorised as 'museum' kind — it's a replica castle that now houses a museum, somewhat misleading.
improvement_hint: Enrich Onomichi municipal scrape to surface Senkoji and the cat alley; add 'historic_district' or 'temple_town' kind tags to increase relevance ranking for those entities.

reasoning: Spanish query about Nachi Falls answered well: primary result is Nachi Falls (Q1365882, Wakayama, waterfall, Natural Monument, Place of Scenic Beauty, soundscape_100). Nachi Primeval Forest, 48 Waterfalls of Nachi, and Seiganto-ji components also present. Heritage designations accurate. Practical gap: no height info (133m), access from Katsuura, or viewing platform info.
missing_data: Waterfall height (133m, tallest single-drop in Japan), Kumano Nachi Taisha shrine (associated), access from JR Kiinagashima/Kii-Katsuura, and best visit season.
improvement_hint: Add height_m field to waterfall entities; cross-link associated shrine (Kumano Nachi Taisha) and Seiganto-ji temple so waterfall queries surface the whole sacred complex.

reasoning: Hindi query about Shiretoko Peninsula in Hokkaido. Response leads with Shiretoko (Q17208861, UNESCO WHS), then Shiretoko Peninsula, National Park, Mt Io, and World Heritage Conservation Center — all correctly Hokkaido. Good breadth. Descriptions thin (null for top item), but UNESCO designation and key entities correct.
missing_data: Wildlife (brown bears, Blakiston's fish owl, Steller's sea eagles), drift ice season (Jan-Mar), Shiretoko Five Lakes access restrictions, guided tour requirements for restricted zones.
improvement_hint: Populate description_en for top Shiretoko entity (currently null); add wildlife and seasonal highlights to the national park description.

reasoning: Query about Dewa Sanzan in Yamagata. The correct entity 出羽三山 (Q1155726, Yamagata, sacred mountain, pilgrimage_route) IS present but ranks #2, behind 出羽島 (Tebajima, Tokushima island) which shares the 出羽 string. Dewazakura Museum and other noise also appear. Target entity found but buried; practical details on the three mountains very thin.
missing_data: Three peaks (Haguro, Gas-san, Yudono), yamabushi ascetic training, Dewa Sanzan Shrine info, bus/hiking access from Tsuruoka, seasonal closure of Gas-san/Yudono (snow).
improvement_hint: Improve tokenisation so '出羽三山' as a compound is ranked above partial matches like '出羽島'; alternatively use search_hybrid which handles compound terms better.

reasoning: Portuguese query about Yakushima Island. Primary result Yakushima (Q242176) is correct: UNESCO WHS, Wilderness Area, Ramsar, prominence=1. National Park, Sugi primeval forest, and lighthouse also present. Factually accurate. Practical gap: no mention of Jomon Sugi (most famous tree), trail difficulty, booking requirements, or accommodation in Anbo/Miyanoura.
missing_data: 縄文杉 (Jomon Cedar) and Wilson Stump as key named attractions; trail length and fitness level; permit/tour booking system; when to avoid typhoon season.
improvement_hint: Add named natural landmarks like 縄文杉 as distinct Wikidata entities rather than only the island-level entry; include trekking-permit or guided-tour requirement flags.

reasoning: Korean query asks about Sado Island broadly. get_japan_heritage returns the UNESCO-listed Sado Gold Mine (top_canonical_heritage) which is correct and useful, but the Japan Heritage items lean heavily toward Shinano River flame pottery (Niigata mainland stories) rather than Sado Island culture. No Sado-specific data about toki (crested ibis), noh theatre, or taraibune boat.
missing_data: Sado Island-specific attractions: toki (ibis) sanctuary, traditional noh theatre, taraibune round boat fishing, gold mine museum visitor info, ferry from Niigata.
improvement_hint: Add island-scoped query option; when prefecture=Niigata and user names '사도', fan out to island-specific municipality data via get_spots with municipality='佐渡市'.

reasoning: Query asks for Tohoku region traditional onsen ryokan. Tool called with prefecture=Akita only — Tohoku is 6 prefectures (Aomori, Iwate, Miyagi, Yamagata, Fukushima, Akita). Results are exclusively 日本秘湯を守る会 members in Akita — valid onsen ryokan data but geographically constrained to 1/6 of Tohoku. No coordinates for any entry, limiting map-based use.
missing_data: Onsen ryokan in Aomori, Iwate, Miyagi, Yamagata, and Fukushima; notable Tohoku onsen towns (Nyuto Onsen, Ginzan Onsen, Naruko, Sukayu); price ranges.
improvement_hint: Implement region fan-out for get_hotels (similar to get_festivals region support) so Tohoku queries automatically cover all 6 prefectures; add coordinates to hito_yu_kai records.

reasoning: Query asks for shukubo (temple lodgings) on the Shikoku Henro pilgrimage. Tool returns Tokushima general hotels: guest houses, onsen ryokan, standard hotels — none tagged as shukubo or temple accommodation. Pilgrimage-specific constraint completely ignored. Togenkyo-Iya is a guesthouse in Iya Valley, not on the 88-temple circuit. No pilgrimage route context.
missing_data: Shukubo (宿坊) tagged properties on the 88-temple circuit; henro trail map; temples 1-23 in Tokushima; walking vs cycling pilgrimage options.
improvement_hint: Add shukubo lodging type to get_hotels taxonomy; tag known temple-stay accommodations at 88 pilgrimage temples; add henro-specific filter option.

reasoning: Chinese query asks for Hokkaido farm experience (農業体験). Tool returns general Hokkaido spots: volcano (Showa-shinzan), Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle — entirely tourist attractions, zero farming or agri-tourism content. The constraint '農業体験' is completely ignored. Complete constraint failure.
missing_data: Farm stay facilities, lavender fields (富良野), asparagus/potato picking farms, dairy farm tours, agri-tourism operators anywhere in Hokkaido.
improvement_hint: Add agri-tourism / farm_stay kind tag; index known farm-experience venues (e.g., Farm Tomita, Tokachi farms) with appropriate kinds so agricultural queries can be answered.

reasoning: Query about traditional pottery in Kyushu; tool returns Saga craft items: Imari-Arita Ware and Karatsu Ware (both METI traditional crafts, 陶磁器 category) with production areas, descriptions, and registration dates. Hakata Ori (weaving) also appears due to Saga's multi-prefecture overlap. see_also_wikidata_venues offers Takeo City Library as a related venue. Solid for pottery topic.
missing_data: Specific kiln (窯元) visit addresses in Arita/Karatsu; pottery museum listings; workshop experience booking; other Kyushu kilns (Hagi, Karatsu town walking map).
improvement_hint: Cross-link craft records to get_spots venue data for the production area towns (Arita, Karatsu) so agents can chain calls to find specific kiln visit locations.

reasoning: Korean query asks about Tohoku festivals; tool called with only Aomori prefecture. Returns Hachinohe Sansha Taisai and Aomori Nebuta — both valid Aomori festivals in Korean. But Tohoku has 6 prefectures with major festivals (Sendai Tanabata, Akita Kanto, Yamagata Hanagasa) not represented. national_heritage fills with unrelated items from Saitama, Toyama. Significant geographic constraint miss.
missing_data: Sendai Tanabata (Miyagi), Akita Kanto (Akita), Yamagata Hanagasa, Morioka Sansa Odori (Iwate), Fukushima Waraji — the full Tohoku 4 major festivals context.
improvement_hint: Implement region fan-out in get_festivals for multi-prefecture regions (Tohoku/Kansai/Kyushu etc.) with a region parameter, similar to how other tools handle region queries.
